org.arakhne.afc.ui.android.property

Class PropertyEditorView

  • java.lang.Object
    • android.view.View
      • android.view.ViewGroup
        • android.widget.LinearLayout
          • org.arakhne.afc.ui.android.property.PropertyEditorView
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    android.graphics.drawable.Drawable.Callback, android.view.accessibility.AccessibilityEventSource, android.view.KeyEvent.Callback, android.view.ViewManager, android.view.ViewParent
    Direct Known Subclasses:
    TablePropertyEditorView

    public abstract class PropertyEditorView
extends android.widget.LinearLayout
    Abstract implementation of a property editor inside a view.

    This view create a set of fields in which the properties of an object can be edited. This implementation does not make any asssumption on the layout of the fields on the screen.

    The labels of the properties, which are displayed on the screen, are retreived from the resources of the Android application. Let say the property myProp. This view tries to retreive the entry string/property_label_myProp in the resources. Because each entry in the resources is prefixed by a package name, a list of package names to consider must be provided. This list is given to the constructor of this view. In addition to this list, this view search in the Android application package (see Context.getPackageName()) and in the package of this ApkLib (see R).

    Version:

    • Constructor Detail

      • PropertyEditorView

        protected PropertyEditorView(android.content.Context context)
        Create a view to edit properties with only the android application package and this ApkLib package in the list of package names.
        Parameters:
        context -

      • PropertyEditorView

        protected PropertyEditorView(android.content.Context context,
                  List<String> packageNames)
        Create a view to edit properties with a list of package names.

        The list of package names is used to retreive the labels of the properties in the Android resources.

        The application package is automatically added at the first position of the given list of package names. The package of this apklib is added at the end of the list.

        Parameters:
        context -
        packageNames - is the list of the package names from which the label resources may be retreived.

    • Method Detail

      • getPropertyLabel

        protected String getPropertyLabel(String propertyName)
        Replies the label for the property with the given name.

        This function search for a string in the Android resources with the name "property_"+propertyName.

        Parameters:
        propertyName - is the name of the property.
        Returns:
        the label or null.

      • isEditable

        public boolean isEditable()
        Replies if the fragment enables to edit the properties.
        Returns:
        true if the properties are editable.

      • setEditable

        public void setEditable(boolean editable)
        Set if the fragment enables to edit the properties.
        Parameters:
        editable - is true if the properties are editable.

      • getEditedObjects

        public Collection<? extends PropertyOwner> getEditedObjects()
        Replies the edited objects.
        Returns:
        the edited objects.

      • setEditedObjects

        public void setEditedObjects(Collection<? extends PropertyOwner> editedObjects)
        Change the edited object.
        Parameters:
        editedObjects -

      • onResetContentView

        protected abstract void onResetContentView()
        Invoked when the content view must be cleared.

        protected abstract android.view.View onCreateTopContentView()
        Invoked when the top-most (the root) content view must be initiated.

      • onCreateFields

        protected abstract void onCreateFields(Collection<? extends PropertyOwner> editedObject)
        Invoked to create the fields of the property editor.
        Parameters:

      • getEditedProperties

        public abstract Map<String,Object> getEditedProperties()
        Replies the properties in the fragment.
        Returns:
        the properties.

      • setPropertiesOf

        public void setPropertiesOf(PropertyOwner owner,
                   Map<String,Object> properties)
        Invoked by the API when the properties of an owner must be set.

        This function shuold be overidden by subclasses to enable specific settings.

        Parameters:
        owner - is the object to change.
        properties - are the properties to set.
